title: Tools Refactor
main_idea: We refactor the concept of the tools sidebar to be a container that loads custom component from a configured directory
reason: Needed tools vary greatly per user. Let's just make it work for anyone with some guided rules, etc.  Tools don't ever need this applications state.
requirements: The tools sidebar becomes a container that loads in a folder of created / built tools into tabs that can be switched.
We create an embedded project, with a structured templated layout, that users can clone and add their own components.
Their will be a build job that outputs them to a configured location, and our app would have a configurationt to know to look there.
We could have an easy toolkit that renders a minimalistic frame of the app, with included tools loaded into the sidebar for testing
ui_notes: We need to consider what it would look like for 2 tools vs 100 tools.  maybe we need an organization structure, and some well designed navigation setup to get between tools.
Maybe we have a favorites list, can have like 5 tools in your favorites bar, and the tabs get smaller as you add, and you have to click a button and nav to open another tool.
open_questions: Which UI UX should we go with?
